1176	`AwayComparison.comparison` — provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,

1183	lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
1184	    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1185	    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
1186	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1187	        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
1188	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1189	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1190	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1191	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1192	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1193	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1194	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1195	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1196	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1197	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1198	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h

972	[Positive-degree exactness of the section {\v C}ech module complex]
973	  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
974	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact}
975	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
976	  With the notation of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, the
977	  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\) is exact in positive
978	  degrees: for every \(p\),
979	  \[
980	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(d^{p+1} : D^{p+1} \to D^{p+2},\;
981	      d^{p+2} : D^{p+2} \to D^{p+3}\bigr).
982	  \]
983	  This is step~(a) of the affine vanishing: the node-by-node exactness of the
984	  un-localised module complex, from which the homology vanishing of the section
985	  {\v C}ech complex (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, step~(c)) is read off.
986	\end{lemma}

988	\begin{proof}
989	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
990	  \leanok
991	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
992	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
993	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the
994	  spanning hypothesis \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)), it
995	  suffices to verify \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p+1}, d^{p+2})\) after
996	  localising at each spanning element \(s_r\). In the localisation at \(s_r\) the
997	  comparison family \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\) identifies the localised
998	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
999	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} (using the localisation
1000	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\) of
1001	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that
1002	  localised differential is \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\), which is in turn the
1003	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial exactness transported along
1004	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\); the contracting homotopy at the fixed index
1005	  \(r\) (where \(s_r\) is invertible) furnishes \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\),
1006	  giving the localised exactness one localisation at a time. Reassembling over the
1007	  spanning family yields the un-localised exactness.
1008	\end{proof}

1174	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1175	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1176	
1177	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1178	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1179	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1180	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1181	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1182	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1183	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1184	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1185	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1186	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1187	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1188	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1189	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1190	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1191	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1192	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1193	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1194	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1195	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1196	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1197	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
